sections.incorrect_information: The input example instructs using <s> and </s> tokens for separation. The provided tokenizer/post-processor uses [CLS] and [SEP] (see tokenizer_config.json), and added tokens expose <|source|>, <|candidate1|>, <|candidate2|>, not <s>/<\s>. Use [CLS]/[SEP] rather than <s>/<\/s>.

The claim that the model performs better than baselines on shorter texts (CommonGen, WMT18) compared to longer summarization is not supported by the paper’s results: gains on CNN/DM (Rouge-1 +6.35%) are comparable or larger than CommonGen (+2.45% CIDEr) and WMT18 (+6.12% BLEU) (Tables 4–6).

The card states MaxLogits is the default aggregation method. However, ranker_config.json sets "inference_mode": "bubble". While the paper notes MaxLogits performs best and is used as the default in experiments, the provided configuration defaults to bubble for inference.
